Navigation

Model Context Protocol

MCP Servers Overview

##### Getting Started

- [Overview](https://docs.replit.com/getting-started/intro-replit)
- Quickstarts

- [Build in ChatGPT](https://docs.replit.com/getting-started/quickstarts/build-in-chatgpt)
- Import


##### Core Concepts

- [How Replit works](https://docs.replit.com/core-concepts/how-replit-works)
- Agent

- Workspace

- Design

- Projects

- Storage

- Integrations

  - [AI](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/replit-ai-integrations)
  - [Connectors](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/integrations)
  - [Open in Replit](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/open-in-replit)
  - Model Context Protocol

    - [Overview](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/overview)
    - [Directory](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/directory)
    - [Install Links](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/install-links)
  - [Figma](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/figma)
  - Debugging Connectors
- Monetization


##### Platforms

- [Mobile App](https://docs.replit.com/platforms/mobile-app)
- [ChatGPT](https://docs.replit.com/platforms/chatgpt)

##### Additional Resources

- [CLUI](https://docs.replit.com/additional-resources/clui-graphical-cli)
- [Cheat Sheet](https://docs.replit.com/additional-resources/cheat-sheet)
- [FAQ](https://docs.replit.com/faq)

Replit Agent can connect to hundreds of external tools and data sources through the [Model Context Protocol (MCP)](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/), an open standard for integrations between AI tooling providers. MCP servers let Agent leverage context and tools from external services, databases, and APIs.Use the **MCP servers** section in the Integrations pane to connect any remote MCP server. Once connected, Agent automatically loads the tools each server makes available and will use them when appropriate.

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/overview\#mcp-servers-section)  MCP servers section

1

[Navigate to header](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/overview#)

Open the MCP servers section

Go to [Integrations Page](https://replit.com/integrations), then scroll down to **MCP Servers for Replit Agent** to view existing servers or add a new one.

2

[Navigate to header](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/overview#)

Add an MCP server

Click **Add MCP server** and provide a recognizable name so Agent can reference the server in chat logs.

![showing the MCP servers section in the integrations pane](https://mintcdn.com/replit/TGnQMe8czfYDHq7u/images/replitai/custom-mcp/add-mcp-button.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=TGnQMe8czfYDHq7u&q=85&s=b32cee6cab4dbbc96139810003f90d5d)

3

[Navigate to header](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/overview#)

Test & Save

Enter the server URL and authentication details, then select **Test & Save**. This will attempt to connect to the server and guide you through the authentication flow if required.

![showing the MCP server setup](https://mintcdn.com/replit/TGnQMe8czfYDHq7u/images/replitai/custom-mcp/test-and-save.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=TGnQMe8czfYDHq7u&q=85&s=c3348dff2cfecd29d0d6bce6d630fc16)

Once saved, the connection appears under **MCP servers** with its connection status. You can remove it at any time from the same pane by clicking the “Manage” button.

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/overview\#use-mcp-tools-in-agent)  Use MCP tools in Agent

After a connection is active, Agent automatically fetches the server’s tool list and makes its capabilities available in chat for all your projects. To get started, just ask the agent to use the MCP server by mentioning the display name you configured during setup.

![showing the MCP server being used](https://mintcdn.com/replit/TGnQMe8czfYDHq7u/images/replitai/custom-mcp/tool-call.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=TGnQMe8czfYDHq7u&q=85&s=66724b66f8599496fe22847a17503cea)

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/overview\#security-protections)  Security protections

All custom MCP traffic passes through Replit’s security scanner. The scanner evaluates tool definitions and planned executions, blocking suspicious or unsafe tools before they run. If a tool is rejected, Agent will inform you about it.

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/overview\#authentication-options)  Authentication options

- **OAuth dynamic client registration**: If the server supports OAuth DCR, Replit automatically detects and registers the client for you.
- **Custom headers**: Define one or more header name and value pairs (for example, `X-API-Key`). Replit includes these headers in every MCP request so your server can verify the caller.

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/overview\#explore-mcp-resources)  Explore MCP resources

- [Browse the MCP directory](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/directory) for featured server integrations.
- [Grab install links](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/install-links) to quickly share tested MCP server connections.

MCP

Learn about MCP in 3 minutes

##### Vibe Coding & Prompting

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3\#why-ai-models-need-mcp-to-connect-with-the-real-world)  Why AI models need MCP to connect with the real world

AI models like Claude and GPT are powerful but limited to what they were trained on. Without access to external tools and data sources, they can’t:

- Access up-to-date information
- Interact with external systems
- Perform actions in the real world
- Work with your private data

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3\#what-mcp-does-the-universal-connector-for-ai-apps)  What MCP does: The universal connector for AI apps

Model Context Protocol (MCP) solves this by creating a universal way for AI to connect to tools and data sources - similar to how USB-C standardized device connections.

![](https://mintcdn.com/replit/0ixNWaRF232g0Gwn/images/tutorials/mcp.avif?fit=max&auto=format&n=0ixNWaRF232g0Gwn&q=85&s=fa8ad7a48e8e22fef036f6ef9c804536)

MCP is a standardized protocol that allows AI models to:

- Access specialized tools and APIs
- Work with private data sources
- Perform actions in the real world
- Connect to other systems seamlessly

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3\#how-mcp-works-understanding-the-key-components)  How MCP works: Understanding the key components

The MCP architecture has three main components:

1. **The Client Side**: AI models like Claude or applications that need to access external tools
2. **The Communication Layer**: The protocol itself that standardizes how requests and responses are formatted
3. **The Server Side**: Programs that provide access to tools, data sources, and specialized capabilities

What's an MCP client?

An MCP client is something like Claude or a command-line interface that connects to a large language model (LLM). It’s the device that needs to plug into external tools or data sources.Examples of MCP clients:

- Claude in the browser
- Command-line interfaces for AI
- Custom applications built with AI SDKs

What's an MCP server?

An MCP server provides tools and capabilities to AI models. Think of it like giving AI a set of specialized tools to solve problems.Examples of what MCP servers enable:

- Accessing specific data sources to answer questions
- Connecting AI to APIs so it can go online
- Enabling video summarization or transcript fetching
- Writing files to your computer
- Making calculations or running code

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3\#mcp-capabilities-that-extend-ai-functionality)  MCP capabilities that extend AI functionality

MCP provides several key features that make it powerful for AI applications:

- **Resources**: Share data and content with AI models
- **Tools**: Let AI models perform actions through your services
- **Prompts**: Create reusable templates for consistent AI interactions
- **Sampling**: Allow your services to request information from AI models
- **Transports**: Connect clients and servers efficiently

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3\#try-mcp-yourself-build-an-ai-tool-in-minutes)  Try MCP yourself: Build an AI tool in minutes

Run MCP in 3 minutes - EVERYTHING you need to know about Model Context Protocol - YouTube

[Photo image of Matt Palmer](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCpe4azYvizhOPP22aSYnxwQ?embeds_referring_euri=https%3A%2F%2Fdocs.replit.com%2F)

Matt Palmer

30.3K subscribers

•Live

1

[Navigate to header](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3#)

Set up an MCP environment

Replit provides Templates that let you experiment with MCP without installing anything. These Templates include all the necessary components to connect AI models to useful tools.To get started quickly:

1. Remix this template: [Learn about MCP](https://replit.com/@matt/Learn-about-MCP?v=1&utm_source=matt&utm_medium=blog&utm_campaign=mcp-in-3)
2. Wait for the environment to load completely
3. You’ll have a ready-to-use MCP setup with no configuration needed

2

[Navigate to header](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3#)

Run a practical example

A simple demonstration shows how MCP enables AI to:

1. Fetch a YouTube video using just a URL
2. Get the content or transcript of that video
3. Write a summary to a file on your system

Try this command in our MCP Template:

Copy

```
llm "Summarize this video https://youtu.be/1qxOcsj1TAg and write the summary to summary.txt"
```

This demonstrates how MCP gives AI models abilities they wouldn’t normally have.

3

[Navigate to header](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3#)

Customize for your needs

Once you understand the basics, you can connect MCP servers to your own data sources or create custom tools that your AI can use to solve specific problems.

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3\#real-world-applications-you-can-build-with-mcp)  Real-world applications you can build with MCP

MCP enables a wide range of powerful AI applications:

- **Customer service systems** that can access company databases to answer specific questions
- **Research assistants** that can search and summarize content from multiple sources
- **Productivity tools** that can interact with your files and applications
- **Content creation tools** that can access media libraries and publishing platforms

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3\#benefits-of-using-mcp-for-your-ai-projects)  Benefits of using MCP for your AI projects

MCP offers three key benefits:

- Ready-to-use integrations your AI can connect to immediately
- The ability to switch between different AI providers without rewriting your connections
- Security features that keep your sensitive data protected

This is transformative for developers building AI applications and for users who want AI that can do more than just generate text.

MCP is an emerging standard with growing support across the AI ecosystem. More tools and integrations are being added regularly.

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/tutorials/mcp-in-3\#next-steps-go-further-with-mcp-development)  Next steps: Go further with MCP development

Explore more MCP examples

- Check out Templates on Replit to experiment without installing anything
- Look through the MCP protocol documentation to understand how it works
- Join the MCP community to see what others are building

Build your own MCP server

- Create custom tools that connect to your data sources
- Develop specialized capabilities for your AI applications
- Share your MCP servers with the community

Resources

- [MCP Protocol Documentation](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/)
- [Anthropic MCP Starter](https://github.com/anthropics/mcp-starter)
- [Everything you need to know about MCP](https://blog.replit.com/everything-you-need-to-know-about-mcp)

MCP might sound technical, but the concept is simple—it’s about giving AI models access to tools and data through a standardized connection. This expands what AI can accomplish and makes building powerful AI applications more accessible to everyone.

web.title: Custom MCP servers in Replit - YouTube
web.description: ... MCP Server: https://amplitude.com/docs/analytics/amplitude-mcp Key Points: 00:00 Custom MCP support announcement for Replit Agent integration ...
web.markdown:
![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi_webp/4fBDCiWn9Eo/maxresdefault.webp)
# [Custom MCP servers in Replit](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4fBDCiWn9Eo)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [Replit](http://www.youtube.com/@replit)
**Uploaded at**: 2025-12-08T09:28:05-08:00
**Published at**: 2025-12-08T09:28:05-08:00
**Length**: 03:43
**Views**: 2194
**Likes**: 52
**Category**: Entertainment

## Description

```
Expand Replit Agent with Custom MCP Servers—Connect Any Tool Instantly

MCP Servers: https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/overview#mcp-servers-overview?utm_source=Matt&utm_medium=youtube&utm_campaign=mcp-servers
Replit Integrations: replit.com/integrations?utm_source=Matt&utm_medium=youtube&utm_campaign=mcp
MCP on Replit Template: https://replit.com/@matt/MCP-on-Replit-TS?utm_source=Matt&utm_medium=youtube&utm_campaign=mcp
Context Awesome MCP Server: https://www.context-awesome.com/
Amplitude MCP Server: https://amplitude.com/docs/analytics/amplitude-mcp

Key Points:
00:00 Custom MCP support announcement for Replit Agent integration ecosystem
01:30 Connect unauthorized MCP servers by copying remote server URL
02:30 OAuth MCP servers: Amplitude analytics integration with account authorization

#MCP #ReplitAgent #AITools #DeveloperTools #Integration #APIAuthentication #RemoteServers

What custom MCP servers would you connect to your Replit Agent first? Share your use case in the comments—we're building this ecosystem together.
```

## Transcript

Quick walk through here. Today we're announcing custom MCP support for Replet agent. And what that means is that you can connect any of your favorite remote MCP servers to Replet agent with or without O. I'm going to walk through what that means and how it works. So on the integrations page, this is replet.com/integrations. Towards the bottom, you'll see MCP servers for replet agent. And now you can add your own custom MCP servers. So we're going to walk through this with two examples. First, we're going to talk about a server that provides access um to a list of resources that is free and openly accessible. And then I'll show you an example of an OOTH MCP server. So, um I found this cool little MCP server called context awesome. You can give your AI agents access to 8,500 awesome lists with curated resources. So, this isn't a good example of like a reference MCP server or a server that helps you find information. But what I'm noticing down here is that they have a remote server. So, we're going to copy that and we're going to go back to our integrations and add a custom MCP server. You'll notice this modal. We're going to continue and add our awesome MCP server. We're going to call it awesome list. And then we're going to test and save that. Since this is an unauthorized MCP server, we're just going to add it to the list. Pretty easy to do. Now, if you go into a Replet app, just any really replet app will do. can say what are some awesome resources for technical writing and reboot agent is going to start working on that with our tools with our MCP servers and what you should notice here in a few seconds is a match of that server. So we can see agent chose an MCP integration it used the tool and now it's going to respond with the context of that tool which is access to a curated list of resources. So, this is a really great pattern if you have documentation, if you have specific tools that you're trying to work with that aren't super well documented um that have remote MCPA servers or custom resources. It's also a really great pattern if you're trying to build your own MCP server with a list of resources or ways that you like doing things. You can expose that to replet agent. And so, we can see uh our search results here uh that agent gave us. Now, another really great pattern is OATH MCP servers. So, we also support on our integrations um OOTH servers. So, if we add a custom MCP server, um now we're going to use another one of my favorite MCP servers, which is the Amplitude MCP server. So, you can see here on the Amplitude documentation, Amplitude is a tool for um data analysis. We could add this MCP server, call it amplitude, and then test and save that. Now, since this is an O MCP server, Rebel's saying, "Hey, we got to authorize this." We'll get a little popup. I'm going to authorize that with my Amplitude account which I'm already logged into and the connection is that easy. So now we have our Amplitude MCP server that we could query with Replet agent as well. So just like that you can add MCP servers to Replet agent. Currently we support remote MCP servers with or without um authentication. One really easy way to deploy your own remote MCP server is using our MCP onrelet template that comes with API key authentication out of the box. So that would be supported in our um integrations pane uh simply by adding a custom MCP with a header and your authentication value. So you can use that API key authentication. Coming soon will be the ability to deploy a server with OOTH directly from within Replet. But this has been MCP support for Replet agent. I'm Matt. Until next time.

Install links let users add your MCP server to Replit with a single click. Share these links in your documentation, README files, or anywhere you want to promote your integration.

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/install-links\#generate-a-link)  Generate a link

Use this form to generate an install link and badge for your MCP server:

Display name

Base URL

Install link

```
https://replit.com/integrations?mcp=eyJkaXNwbGF5TmFtZSI6Ik15IE1DUCBTZXJ2ZXIiLCJiYXNlVXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9leGFtcGxlLmNvbS9tY3AifQ==
```

Badge preview

[![Add to Replit badge](https://replit.com/badge?caption=Add%20to%20Replit)](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/install-links#)

Badge markdown

```
[![Add to Replit](https://replit.com/badge?caption=Add%20to%20Replit)](https://replit.com/integrations?mcp=eyJkaXNwbGF5TmFtZSI6Ik15IE1DUCBTZXJ2ZXIiLCJiYXNlVXJsIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9leGFtcGxlLmNvbS9tY3AifQ==)
```

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/install-links\#link-format)  Link format

Install links use a base64-encoded JSON payload containing your server configuration:

Copy

```
https://replit.com/integrations?mcp={payload}
```

The payload includes:

- **displayName**: The name shown to users during installation
- **baseUrl**: Your MCP server’s HTTPS endpoint
- **headers**: Optional authentication headers

Copy

```
{
  "displayName": "My MCP Server",
  "baseUrl": "https://example.com/mcp",
  "headers": [\
    {\
      "key": "Authorization",\
      "value": "Bearer your-token"\
    }\
  ]
}
```

## [​](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/install-links\#using-badges)  Using badges

Add a clickable badge to your README or documentation that installs your MCP server when clicked.

Copy

```
[![Install My MCP](https://replit.com/badge?caption=Install%20My%20MCP)](https://replit.com/integrations?mcp={payload})
```

![Install MCP Badge](https://replit.com/badge?caption=Install%20MCP%20Server)

You can customize the badge caption (maximum 30 characters) by URL-encoding the text in the `caption` parameter.

Was this page helpful?

YesNo

\\
Next](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/figma)

Ctrl+I

- [Using badges](https://docs.replit.com/replitai/mcp/install-links#using-badges)

web.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP): A Comprehensive Guide - Replit Blog
web.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a standard way to connect AI models to data sources and tools. It allows AI to access information and capabilities beyond what ...
web.markdown:
[Blog](https://blog.replit.com/)

- [AI](https://blog.replit.com/category/ai)

# Everything you need to know about MCP

![](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/bj34pdbp/migration/2c5236761e95ccb8551ea7f96cb34bea4d8e02b0-3840x2160.jpg?w=3840&q=75&fit=clip&auto=format)

Sat, Mar 8, 2025

Updated at:

Mon, Jul 14, 2025

![Matt Palmer](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/bj34pdbp/migration/8133f786ee981fb7b6bc8ad4c25d0c3a5ef723f7-2336x2336.jpg)

Matt Palmer

Run MCP in 3 minutes - EVERYTHING you need to know about Model Context Protocol - YouTube

Tap to unmute

[Run MCP in 3 minutes - EVERYTHING you need to know about Model Context Protocol](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=zyDm-MJgDOA) [Matt Palmer](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCpe4azYvizhOPP22aSYnxwQ)

![thumbnail-image](https://yt3.ggpht.com/ytc/AIdro_nYEUSob4RRDnbSaIFZGNETqdtclaW-ibz4m1gADjphCCHH=s68-c-k-c0x00ffffff-no-rj)

Matt Palmer30.3K subscribers

_You can find the 3-min quickstart from the video [here](https://replit.com/@matt/Learn-about-MCP?v=1&utm_source=matt&utm_medium=blog&utm_campaign=mcp-in-3)_

## What is MCP?

MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a standard way to connect AI models to data sources and tools. It allows AI to access information and capabilities beyond what they were originally trained on.

![](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/bj34pdbp/migration/2d4bba7266125e91aecd157477d2075a2da66f8b-3519x1934.png?w=3840&q=100&fit=max&auto=format)

MCP works like a universal connector for AI systems. Just as standard ports let different devices work together, MCP lets different AI models connect to the same tools and data sources. This means developers can build a tool once and have it work with any AI model that supports MCP. It's like the USB-C port of AI applications.

AI models are good at generating content and reasoning, but they're limited by their training data. MCP solves this problem by giving them access to external resources when they need them.

## How MCP Works

MCP uses a client-server design where applications can connect to multiple resources. The system has three main parts:

### The Client Side: Making Requests

MCP Clients/Hosts are where requests begin. These include:

- AI models like Claude or GPT that need external tools
- Applications like Claude Desktop or code editors
- Any system that connects an AI model to external resources

The client sends requests through MCP to access tools or information—similar to how your web browser requests a webpage.

### The Communication Layer: The Standard Protocol

The _Protocol_ itself is the core of MCP. This standard:

- Defines the format for requests and responses
- Makes different models and tools compatible
- Handles security, errors, and data formatting

This protocol ensures all parts of the system can work together regardless of which AI model or tool is being used.

### The Server Side: Providing Resources

_MCP Servers_ connect to the resources AI models need. These lightweight programs:

- Make specific capabilities available through the standard protocol
- Provide access to tools and data
- Connect to databases for information
- Work with services like YouTube, weather data, or stock prices
- Access files for reading and writing
- Perform specialized tasks

Servers receive requests, perform the needed actions, and send results back to the AI model.

This design creates a system where AI models can access the digital world when needed. Let's look at why this matters in practice.

## Why MCP Matters

Without MCP, AI models can only use what they learned during training. They can't:

- Get current information from the internet
- Query databases for specific answers
- Use specialized services like video processing
- Save information to files
- Access external tools that extend their abilities

MCP bridges this gap by giving models access to tools beyond themselves. This turns AI from isolated systems into connected applications that can solve real problems.

MCP offers three key benefits:

- Ready-to-use integrations your AI can connect to immediately
- The ability to switch between different AI providers without rewriting your connections
- Security features that keep your sensitive data protected

## MCP in Action

Here's a simple example from the video:

- An AI model receives a YouTube URL
- Through MCP, it connects to a service that gets YouTube transcripts
- The service returns the transcript to the model
- The model summarizes the content
- Using another MCP service, it saves the summary to a file

Without MCP, each step would require custom code and integration. With MCP, it's a standard process that works across different AI systems.

## Getting Started with MCP

Our [MCP Template](https://replit.com/@matt/Learn-about-MCP?v=1&utm_source=matt&utm_medium=blog&utm_campaign=mcp-in-3) lets you get started in minutes. Simply:

- Remix the template
- Paste in your OpenAI API key
- Run the command

```typescript
llm "Summarize this video https://youtu.be/1qxOcsj1TAg and write the summary to summary.txt"
```

Most people can set up in under five minutes. MCP supports multiple programming languages including Python, TypeScript, Java, and others. Whether you're creating new services or using existing ones, there's a path for you.

## Core Capabilities

MCP provides several key features:

- Resources: Share data and content with AI models
- Prompts: Create reusable templates for consistent AI interactions
- Tools: Let AI models perform actions through your services
- Sampling: Allow your services to request information from AI models
- Transports: Connect clients and servers efficiently

## The Future of AI Integration

MCP represents an important step forward in AI development. It enables:

- AI applications that can interact with real-world systems
- Easier development across different AI models
- Standard ways to extend AI capabilities
- A growing collection of specialized tools any AI can use

As AI continues to develop, connecting models to external tools and data will become increasingly important. MCP provides the foundation for this integration.

## Wrapping Up

MCP changes how we think about AI capabilities by connecting models to specialized tools and resources.

Understanding MCP helps you see how AI can work with the digital world around it. It's about making AI more useful by giving it access to the tools and information it needs.

With our platform, you can build AI applications with expanded capabilities. I look forward to seeing what you'll create.

Want to learn more? Check out the [official MCP documentation](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/introduction) for tutorials, examples, and guides on building with MCP.

More

- [![](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/bj34pdbp/migration/5789f6e7af051646efeb4101bf1017aa89a4d646-1280x720.jpg?w=3840&q=75&fit=clip&auto=format)\\
\\
Fri, Feb 13, 2026 **Vibe Coding Data Apps with Replit + Snowflake - Part 2 \[webinar\]** \\
\\
Summary\\
\\
Snowflake’s Vino Duraisamy and Replit’s Manny Bernabe return to turn simple dashboards into working data tools.\\
\\
Live forecasting and drilldowns explain why metrics move, not just what changed on your Snowflake dashboard.\\
\\
Custom Slack alerts fire when revenue crosses key thresholds so teams catch spikes, drops, and odd days quickly.\\
\\
An embedded AI data assistant answers plain-English questions and shows the exact SQL it ran for every query.\\
\\
Replit handles security scanning, auth, and hosting so you can ship Snowflake-powered data apps in minutes.](https://blog.replit.com/replit-and-snowflake-webinar-pt2)
- [![](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/bj34pdbp/migration/979d1df1aae3e6b0553b5f834bcbc45f645b603e-1200x675.jpg?w=3840&q=75&fit=clip&auto=format)\\
\\
Fri, Jan 30, 2026 **Vibe Coding Data Apps with Replit + Snowflake \[webinar\]** \\
\\
Summary\\
\\
Snowflake developer advocate Vino Duraisamy joins Replit Community Manager Manny Bernabe for another informative live webinar.\\
\\
Replit's Snowflake connector enables builders to vibe code custom data applications without writing code manually.\\
\\
The connector provides secure, read-only access to Snowflake databases, eliminating complex authentication and setup processes.\\
\\
Builders can iterate on dashboards and reports using natural language prompts in plan and build modes.\\
\\
Publishing production-ready apps takes minutes with built-in security scanning, authentication, and deployment features all included.](https://blog.replit.com/replit-and-snowflake-webinar)
- [![](https://cdn.sanity.io/images/bj34pdbp/migration/2b186865442a22d401c556741f1e818d16fa47a2-1280x720.jpg?w=3840&q=75&fit=clip&auto=format)\\
\\
Fri, Jan 23, 2026 **Building a Private Bookmark Manager and Browser Extension in Replit \[webinar\]** \\
\\
Summary\\
\\
Replit hosted a livestream build session showing how to create a private bookmark manager with a Chrome extension from scratch using Replit Agent in under two hours.\\
\\
The team demonstrated how to write effective prompts, troubleshoot issues, and add AI-powered features like content summarization using OpenAI integration with minimal manual coding.\\
\\
Building the initial functional app and extension cost around five dollars using Replit Agent, with additional costs for feature iterations and enhancements throughout the session.\\
\\
The livestream showcased real-world debugging and problem-solving, including handling database migrations, security vulnerabilities, and browser extension authentication flows as they occurred.\\
\\
The start of 2026 is proving to be an exciting time for builders on Replit. In a recent livestream, community managers Manny Bernable and Raymmnar walked over 1,000 viewers through building a complete bookmark management application with a Chrome extension—live and unscripted—using Replit Agent.](https://blog.replit.com/bookmark-manager-browser-extension)
